architecture.summary: SeLaR is a training-free latent reasoning framework that selectively activates soft embeddings during language model decoding based on token-level entropy. At each step, the model computes normalized entropy over top-k tokens. If entropy falls below a learned threshold (deterministic step), standard discrete token decoding is used. If entropy exceeds the threshold (exploratory step), a soft embedding is constructed as a probability-weighted mixture of top-k token embeddings and passed to the next layer. An entropy-aware contrastive regularization component then pushes this soft embedding away from the dominant token's direction proportionally to entropy magnitude, sustaining exploration of multiple reasoning paths.

biblio.title: SeLaR: Selective Latent Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: SeLaR achieves the highest average accuracy, improving upon CoT (Sampling) by +0.75%, +3.88%, and +2.45% on Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-8B, and Qwen3-32B, respectively.
On Qwen3-8B, SeLaR improves AIME 2024 from 76.67% to 83.33% (+6.66%) and AIME 2025 from 66.67% to 80.00% (+13.33%).
Removing selective activation (i.e., applying soft embeddings globally at every step) leads to an average performance drop of 5.19% (from 83.56% to 78.37%).

Removing contrastive regularization results in an average performance drop of 7.82% (from 83.56% to 75.74%), with AIME 2024 dropping from 83.33% to 70.00% and AIME 2025 dropping from 80.00% to 60.00%.

Exploratory steps (high entropy) account for 6.2%–13.8% of total reasoning tokens, averaging 10.0%, confirming that selective activation targets approximately one in ten tokens where exploration is most beneficial.

SeLaR's TPCA (Tokens per Correct Answer) is higher than CoT on GPQA, attributed to GPQA's knowledge-intensive nature, where latent exploration at uncertain decoding steps provides little benefit when correct answers depend on domain recall rather than multi-step reasoning.

Logit lens analysis shows that without contrastive regularization, the forward pass progressively collapses onto the top-1 trajectory and suppresses the top-2 alternative; with contrastive regularization, both top-1 and top-2 overlaps remain substantially non-zero and comparable in deep layers, confirming genuine sustained exploration of latent reasoning paths.

SeLaR yields larger improvements on base models with higher confidence (e.g., Qwen3-8B) than on those with lower confidence (e.g., DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B). Less confident models exhibit higher entropy more frequently, triggering excessive exploratory steps.
